headline: Stanley Black And Decker Expansion In Texas Tests Supply Chain Ambitions
summary: Stanley Black & Decker (NYSE:SWK) is investing more than US$4 million to expand its Mission, Texas manufacturing facility. The project is expected to add nearly 100 new jobs at the site. The expansion reflects an increased commitment to U.S. manufacturing and the South Texas region. For readers following NYSE:SWK, this move centers on the company's core tools and outdoor products manufacturing footprint. Expanding the Mission, Texas facility ties directly to its role as a major producer of...

headline: Stanley Black & Decker’s Texas Expansion Might Change The Case For Investing In SWK
summary: Stanley Black & Decker recently invested over US$4,000,000 to expand its Mission, Texas manufacturing facility, adding nearly 100 new jobs and retaining 150 positions at a key DEWALT production hub. This expansion underlines the company’s ongoing commitment to U.S. manufacturing capacity, even as softer organic revenue and EPS trends highlight operational and competitive pressures. Next, we’ll examine how this manufacturing expansion in Mission, Texas interacts with Stanley Black & Decker’s...
